title: Creation of Adam
description: Iconic fresco depicting God giving life to Adam, the first man. Part of the Sistine Chapel ceiling, illustrating Genesis 2:7.
attribution: Michelangelo, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Deluge
description: Dramatic depiction of the Great Flood from Genesis, showing humanity overwhelmed by divine judgment as the waters rise.
attribution: Michelangelo, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Moses Breaking the Tablets of the Law
description: Rembrandt's powerful depiction of Moses shattering the stone tablets upon discovering the Israelites worshipping the golden calf (Exodus 32).
attribution: Rembrandt, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Finding of Moses
description: Pharaoh's daughter discovers the infant Moses among the reeds of the Nile, a pivotal moment in the Exodus narrative.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Levites Bearing the Ark
description: Doré's engraving of the Levitical priests carrying the Ark of the Covenant through the wilderness, central to Leviticus and Numbers.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Brazen Serpent
description: The Israelites look upon the bronze serpent raised by Moses in the wilderness for healing, as described in Numbers 21:8-9.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Moses Viewing the Promised Land
description: Moses stands on Mount Nebo gazing upon the Promised Land he will never enter, the poignant finale of Deuteronomy.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Walls of Jericho Fall Down
description: The dramatic collapse of Jericho's walls as the Israelites march and blow trumpets, fulfilling God's promise to Joshua.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Samson Destroying the Temple
description: Samson brings down the Philistine temple upon himself and his captors, the climax of his story in Judges 16.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Ruth and Boaz
description: Ruth gleans in the fields of Boaz, a scene of loyalty and providence from the Book of Ruth.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: David and Goliath
description: The young shepherd David faces the Philistine giant Goliath with only a sling and stones, a defining moment of 1 Samuel.
attribution: Caravaggio, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Anointing of David
description: David is anointed king over Israel, fulfilling the prophecy and the promise of a new era under God's chosen ruler.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Judgment of Solomon
description: Raphael's depiction of Solomon's legendary wisdom in resolving the dispute between two mothers claiming the same child (1 Kings 3:16-28).
attribution: Raphael, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Elijah Taken Up in a Chariot of Fire
description: The prophet Elijah ascends to heaven in a whirlwind with a chariot of fire, as witnessed by Elisha (2 Kings 2:11).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Destruction of the Temple of Jerusalem
description: Francesco Hayez's painting of the destruction of Solomon's Temple, depicting the catastrophic event recorded in 2 Chronicles.
attribution: Francesco Hayez, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Ezra Reading the Law
description: Doré's engraving of Ezra reading the Book of the Law before the assembled people of Israel after the return from exile.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Nehemiah Views the Ruins of Jerusalem
description: Nehemiah surveys the broken walls of Jerusalem by night, steeling his resolve to rebuild the holy city.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Esther Before the King
description: Queen Esther approaches King Ahasuerus to plead for her people, risking her life in a defining act of courage.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Job and His Friends
description: Job sits in anguish among the ashes while his three friends attempt to comfort him in his suffering.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: King David Playing the Harp
description: David, the sweet psalmist of Israel, plays his harp in devotion to God — the traditional image of the Psalms' author.
attribution: Gerard van Honthorst, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: King Solomon in Old Age
description: A contemplative portrait of Solomon, traditionally credited as the author of Proverbs, reflecting on wisdom and folly.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Vanitas Still Life
description: A meditation on the transience of earthly pleasures and the inevitability of death, echoing Ecclesiastes' 'Vanity of vanities.'
attribution: Pieter Claesz, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Song of Solomon
description: A lush depiction of the lovers from the Song of Songs, celebrating the beauty of romantic love as described in the biblical poem.
attribution: Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Vision of Isaiah
description: Isaiah's prophetic vision of the Lord seated on a high throne with seraphim, as described in Isaiah chapter 6.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Jeremiah Lamenting the Destruction of Jerusalem
description: Rembrandt's haunting portrait of the weeping prophet mourning over Jerusalem's fall, capturing Jeremiah's deep sorrow.
attribution: Rembrandt, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Destruction of Jerusalem
description: The devastating fall of Jerusalem and its temple, the catastrophe mourned throughout the Book of Lamentations.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Vision of Ezekiel
description: Raphael's masterful rendering of Ezekiel's vision of God enthroned among the four living creatures (Ezekiel 1).
attribution: Raphael, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Daniel in the Lions' Den
description: Daniel stands unharmed among the lions, his faith vindicated as God sends an angel to shut the lions' mouths (Daniel 6).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Hosea and Gomer
description: The prophet Hosea's marriage to the unfaithful Gomer, a living parable of God's steadfast love for wayward Israel.
attribution: Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Plague of Locusts
description: A devastating swarm of locusts darkens the sky, the central apocalyptic image from the prophecy of Joel.
attribution: Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Prophet Amos
description: Amos, the shepherd-prophet from Tekoa, delivers God's message of justice to the nations surrounding Israel.
attribution: Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Jonah Cast into the Sea
description: Sailors throw the prophet Jonah overboard into the raging storm, where a great fish awaits to swallow him (Jonah 1:15).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Prophet Micah
description: Micah proclaims God's word, prophesying the birthplace of the Messiah in Bethlehem (Micah 5:2).
attribution: Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Nativity
description: Caravaggio's intimate chiaroscuro painting of the birth of Christ, with the Holy Family bathed in divine light.
attribution: Caravaggio, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Sermon on the Mount
description: Christ delivers the Beatitudes and core teachings to the multitudes gathered on the mountainside (Matthew 5-7).
attribution: Carl Bloch, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Baptism of Christ
description: John the Baptist baptizes Jesus in the Jordan River as the Holy Spirit descends like a dove (Mark 1:9-11).
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Prodigal Son
description: Rembrandt's masterpiece depicting the father embracing his lost son who has returned home, illustrating Luke 15:11-32.
attribution: Rembrandt, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Annunciation
description: The angel Gabriel announces to Mary that she will bear the Son of God, the opening scene of Luke's Gospel.
attribution: Raphael, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Last Supper
description: Leonardo da Vinci's iconic depiction of Jesus sharing bread and wine with his disciples on the night before his crucifixion (John 13).
attribution: Leonardo da Vinci, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Crucifixion
description: Raphael's rendering of the crucifixion of Christ, the central event of the Gospel narrative described in John 19.
attribution: Raphael, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Conversion of Saint Paul
description: Caravaggio's dramatic depiction of Saul struck down by divine light on the road to Damascus, his conversion moment (Acts 9).
attribution: Caravaggio, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Saint Paul Writing His Epistles
description: Rembrandt's contemplative portrait of the Apostle Paul composing his letters, including his Epistle to the Romans.
attribution: Rembrandt, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: Saint Paul Preaching in Athens
description: Raphael's tapestry cartoon of Paul addressing the Athenian philosophers on Mars Hill about the 'unknown god' (Acts 17).
attribution: Raphael, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The Four Horsemen of the Apocalypse
description: Dürer's iconic woodcut of the four horsemen riding forth — Conquest, War, Famine, and Death — from Revelation 6.
attribution: Albrecht Dürer, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons

title: The New Jerusalem
description: A glorious vision of the heavenly city descending from God, the culmination of biblical prophecy in Revelation 21.
attribution: Gustave Doré, Public domain, via Wikimedia Commons
